source: CIS AWS COMPUTE SERVICES BENCHMARK V1.1.0
title: Ensure customer-managed keys are used to encrypt AWS Fargate ephemeral storage data for Amazon ECS

input_row.source: CIS AWS COMPUTE SERVICES BENCHMARK V1.1.0
input_row.title: Ensure customer-managed keys are used to encrypt AWS Fargate ephemeral storage data for Amazon ECS
input_row.description: Use customer-managed AWS KMS keys to encrypt AWS Fargate ephemeral storage data for on Amazon ECS, ensuring that sensitive data remains protected during task execution.
input_row.rationale: Customer-managed KMS keys offer enhanced control over encryption, including key rotation, access policies, and audit trails. Impact: There are costs and configuration overhead associated with setting up and managing customer-managed keys.
input_row.audit: From Console: 1. Login to the ECS console using https://console.aws.amazon.com/ecs/. 2. In the left panel, click Clusters. 3. Click the name of a cluster. 4. Ensure that Fargate ephemeral storage is not set to -. 5. Repeat steps 1-4 for each ECS cluster. From Command Line: Run the following command to list clusters: aws ecs list-clusters Run the following command to view the Fargate ephemeral storage KMS key ID configured for a cluster: aws ecs describe-clusters --clusters <cluster-arn> --include CONFIGURATIONS - -query 'clusters[*].configuration.managedStorageConfiguration.fargateEphemeralStorag eKmsKeyId' Ensure the command returns a customer-managed KMS key ARN. Repeat for each cluster.
input_row.remediation: From Console: 1. Login to the ECS console using https://console.aws.amazon.com/ecs/. 2. In the left panel, click Clusters. 3. Click the name of a cluster. 4. Click Update cluster. 5. Expand the Encryption section. 6. Under Fargate ephemeral storage, select a customer-managed KMS key. Note: Ensure the KMS key has appropriate Fargate service permissions. 7. Click Update. 8. Repeat steps 1-7 for each ECS cluster requiring remediation. Default Value: AWS Fargate ephemeral storage data is encrypted by default. References: 1. https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonECS/latest/developerguide/fargate- storage-encryption.html 2. https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonECS/latest/developerguide/fargate-create- storage-key.html 3. https://awscli.amazonaws.com/v2/documentation/api/2.0.33/reference/ecs/list- clusters.html 4. https://awscli.amazonaws.com/v2/documentation/api/2.0.33/reference/ecs/descri be-clusters.html
input_row.impact: There are costs and configuration overhead associated with setting up and managing customer-managed keys.

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**APPROACH DECISION:**
- The audit can be performed using AWS APIs, the boto3 SDK, or AWS CLI commands as described.
- The audit field provides CLI commands to verify the configuration, indicating that it can be checked programmatically.
- AWS ECS and KMS services expose necessary APIs to verify encryption settings for Fargate ephemeral storage.

**PROGRAM NAME:** aws_ecs_cluster_fargate_ephemeral_storage_kms_key_configured

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
- Use the AWS CLI command `aws ecs list-clusters` to enumerate all ECS clusters.
- For each cluster, use `aws ecs describe-clusters --clusters <cluster-arn> --include CONFIGURATIONS` to retrieve the cluster configuration.
- Specifically, validate the `managedStorageConfiguration.fargateEphemeralStorageKmsKeyId` field to ensure it is set to a customer-managed KMS key ARN.
- A pass condition is when the `fargateEphemeralStorageKmsKeyId` is present and corresponds to a valid customer-managed KMS key ARN.
- A fail condition occurs if the `fargateEphemeralStorageKmsKeyId` is missing, set to default, or not a customer-managed key.
- This approach can be implemented using the boto3 SDK by leveraging `boto3.client('ecs').list_clusters()` and `boto3.client('ecs').describe_clusters()` methods for automation.
- The configuration should be checked across all relevant regions and accounts to ensure comprehensive coverage.
